items.title: AMD's excellent Radeon RX 9070 with 16 GB of VRAM hits all-time low pricing — PowerColor Hellhound variant is 23% off list price
items.description: If you've been looking for a GPU upgrade but were disappointed by the ongoing market conditions, a small window of opportunity has just sprung open. AMD's fantastic RX 9070 is on sale for just $554, discounted $165 from its typical retail price. It's a very capable 1440p card that can even game at 4K with enough upscaling.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Thanks to the AI boom causing supply shortages for so many components, it's become a uniquely difficult time to build a PC right now. Indeed, RAM and storage are exorbitantly priced, but even GPUs have gotten more expensive in the past few months. You can still get a good deal here and there, and we've spotted one for you that's hard to miss — AMD's RX 9070 for just $554 on Amazon, down $165 from its typical ask. Check out this deal on Amazon 1440p beastSave 23% ($165.96)PowerColor Hellhound RX 9070 : was $719.99 now $554.03 at AmazonAll-time low price AMD's Radeon RX 9070 is a capable card across almost every resolution. Expect solid 1080p and 1440p performance, with 4K at steady framerates with upscaling. Equipped with 16GB of VRAM, the RX 9070 can run just about every modern title. and even excell in productivity tasks.View Deal This is not the cheapest RX 9070 we've ever seen, but it's close, and it's certainly the most affordable one at this moment. The card itself needs no introduction; we've placed it as the number two pick in our best GPUs roundup for a reason. The 9070 is often outshone by the RX 9070 XT, but don't sleep on this card's value. It can stand its ground against the RTX 5070, delivering buttery-smooth 1440p performance across any game.Featuring 56 Compute Units, paired with 16GB of GDDR6 VRAM across a 256-bit wide bus, the RX 9070 has the same memory bandwidth as the 9070 XT. PowerColor's Hellhound variant has a 12-layer PCB with an 8+1 phase power design, and a dedicated 3+2 phase VRM for the memory. The company is also using PTM7950 on the core, and there's a dual BIOS switch that alternates between an OC and a silent profile. The card doesn't have any RGB because it's going for a sleek and minimal aesthetic.The RX 9070 actually beats the RTX 5070 in rasterization across all resolutions we tested, almost matching the last-gen RX 7900 XT flagship. Similarly, Nvidia's RTX 4070 Ti is weaker than the 9070 overall, while the 4070 Ti Super barely bests it. The story changes when it comes to ray tracing; AMD's offering is definitely better than its previous-gen architecture, but it still falls behind the Green Team, with the 9070 slotting right below the 4070 Super for ray traced workloads. Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) In our review of AMD's 90-class cards this generation, we praised their exceptional price-to-performance ratio, while cutting points for inferior software support. Features like DLSS and RTX HDR remain Nvidia exclusives that might be non-negotiable for some. But for just $554 on Amazon right now, a midrange beast like the RX 9070 is a great deal for someone just looking to play their favorite games during the AI boom. With how uncertain the supply has been for these SKUs, grab yours quick before it sells out. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, Gaming Chair, Best Wi-Fi Routers, Best Motherboard, or CPU Deals pages.

items.titleZh: AMD优秀款Radeon RX 9070搭载16GB显存价格跌至历史低点——撼讯暗黑犬版比建议零售价低23%
items.descriptionZh:
AI热潮导致PC组件普遍短缺，内存、存储和显卡价格飙升，但AMD Radeon RX 9070显卡此时迎来了一波罕见降价。在亚马逊上，PowerColor Hellhound RX 9070售价仅为554美元，较原价719.99美元直降约23%，创下历史新低。这款显卡在1080p和1440p分辨率下表现稳健，配合升频技术也能在4K下维持稳定帧率，16GB GDDR6显存足以流畅运行几乎所有现代游戏及生产力任务。

从核心技术看，RX 9070搭载了56个计算单元（CU），配备256-bit位宽的16GB GDDR6显存，显存带宽与更高端的RX 9070 XT完全一致。PowerColor的Hellhound定制版采用12层PCB板，供电部分为8+1相核心供电加3+2相显存VRM设计，核心导热材料使用了PTM7950相变导热垫，并配有双BIOS开关，可在超频和静音模式间切换。该卡刻意取消了RGB灯效，走极简低调风格。

性能参数方面，在光栅化渲染测试中，RX 9070在全部测试分辨率下均击败RTX 5070，甚至接近上代旗舰RX 7900 XT；相较于NVIDIA的RTX 4070 Ti整体更强，仅略逊于RTX 4070 Ti Super。但在光追性能上，尽管比AMD前代架构有明显进步，仍落后于NVIDIA阵营，大致相当于RTX 4070 Super以下水平。整体而言，RX 9070在本代AMD 90系列显卡中展现出卓越的性价比，但软件生态（如DLSS、RTX HDR等NVIDIA独家功能）仍是短板。

对于在AI热潮中单纯想畅玩主流游戏的玩家而言，这款中端猛兽目前仅554美元的价格极具吸引力。考虑到该型号供货仍不稳定，建议有意向者尽快入手。更多硬件优惠可参阅专业网站的其他显卡、存储、显示器等折扣专题。

items.title: China's Hanyuan-2 debuts as 'world's first' dual-core quantum computer — 200-qubit claims incredible power efficiency, but lacks critical performance benchmarks
items.description: CAS Cold Atom Technology, a Wuhan-based firm affiliated with the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS), unveiled what it calls the world's first dual-core quantum computer.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter CAS Cold Atom Technology, a Wuhan-based firm affiliated with the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS), unveiled what it claims is the world's first dual-core quantum computer, according to a report from state-owned publication Science and Technology Daily. The system, called Hanyuan-2, pairs two independent neutral atom arrays inside a single cabinet-sized machine, totaling 200 qubits built from 100 rubidium-85 and 100 rubidium-87 atoms.The company said the twin cores can either run in parallel to split workloads or operate in a "one main and one auxiliary" configuration, where the second array handles real-time error correction while the first executes computations. Ge Guiguo, a senior expert at CAS Cold Atom Technology, told Science and Technology Daily that the system represents the first time a quantum processor has moved from single-core to dual-core architecture.Hanyuan-2 is built on neutral atom technology, which traps uncharged atoms using laser arrays to cool and manipulate individual neutral atoms as qubits. CAS Cold Atom Technology's general manager, Tang Biao, said the machine uses a compact, cabinet-style integrated design with a small laser cooling system, with total power consumption sitting below 7 kilowatts.Latest Videos From You may like China announces CPU-only exascale supercomputer with 47,000 homemade processors, record 2 Exaflops of performance without GPUs The future of Quantum computing — the tech, companies, and roadmaps that map out a coherent quantum future Quantum photonics roadmap — how Xanadu and PsiQuantum are looking to transfer qubits through beams of light 200 qubits places Hanyaun-2 well behind the West’s leading neutral atom systems. Atom Computing, for example, demonstrated a 1,180-qubit neutral atom array back in 2023 and has since partnered with Microsoft to deliver error-corrected logical qubits on commercial hardware, while QuEra has delivered error-correction-ready machines to Japan's National Institute of Information and Communications Technology and secured over $230 million in new capital through 2025.Metrics and peer-reviewed papers lackingCrucially, both these Western firms have published metrics like gate fidelity, coherence time, and error rate data for their systems, while CAS Cold Atom Technology has disclosed none of these metrics for Hanyuan-2. No peer-reviewed paper accompanied the announcement either, and, as is usually the case with similar announcements coming out of China, all reporting traces back to Chinese state-affiliated outlets.The use of "dual-core" nomenclature also draws a deliberate parallel to classical multi-core CPUs, but the underlying concept is closer to modular quantum computing, an approach Western companies are already pursuing at larger scales. IBM has focused on linking superconducting processors through classical and quantum interconnect, and QuEra and Pasqal are scaling single arrays while developing inter-module connectivity. Atom Computing and Microsoft are building integrated systems designed around networked quantum processors.CAS Cold Atom Technology's approach is more tightly integrated than a networked architecture, placing both arrays inside one machine. Whether that confers a practical advantage over scaling a single, larger array remains an open question, and one that published benchmarks would help answer.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsHanyuan-2 follows the delivery of the company's first-generation system, Hanyuan-1, though technical specifications for that machine are also limited. items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 中国“汉源-2”亮相：号称“全球首台”双核量子计算机，200量子比特宣称能效惊人，但关键性能基准缺失
items.descriptionZh:
中国科学院旗下武汉企业CAS冷原子科技（CAS Cold Atom Technology）近日发布了全球首台双核量子计算机“汉源2号”（Hanyuan-2）。这款系统将两个独立的中性原子阵列集成在一个机柜大小的机器中，共使用200个量子比特——其中100个由铷85原子构成，另外100个由铷87原子构成，实现了双核架构的协同工作。

从技术原理看，汉源2号采用中性原子技术，通过激光阵列捕获不带电的中性原子，并对其进行冷却和操控，使每个原子成为一个量子比特。与传统需要极低温环境的超导量子计算机不同，中性原子系统可在更温和的条件下运行。汉源2号采用了紧凑的机柜式一体化设计，配备了小型激光冷却系统，整机功耗低于7千瓦，在工程集成度上实现了突破。

双核设计的创新在于两种工作模式：一是“并行模式”，两个核心可同时处理不同任务，从而拆分计算负载；二是“一主一辅”模式，一个阵列负责执行主要量子计算，另一个阵列实时进行错误修正，辅助提升计算的可靠性。CAS冷原子科技高级专家葛贵国表示，这是量子处理器首次从单核架构迈向双核架构，意味着量子计算机的系统设计思路开始借鉴经典CPU的演进路径。

然而，在性能参数上，汉源2号与西方领先的中性原子量子系统存在明显差距。例如，美国Atom Computing公司早在2023年就展示了1180量子比特的中性原子阵列，并与微软合作在商用硬件上实现纠错逻辑量子比特；另一家知名公司QuEra已向日本国家信息与通信技术研究所交付了具备纠错能力的机器，并在2025年前累计融资超过2.3亿美元。更重要的是，上述西方企业均已公开其系统的关键指标，如门保真度、相干时间和错误率等数据，但CAS冷原子科技在发布汉源2号时并未披露任何此类指标，也没有提供经过同行评议的学术论文。目前关于该系统的所有报道均来自中国官方媒体，缺乏独立第三方验证。

从架构理念来看，“双核”命名有明显的向经典多核CPU致敬的意图，但其底层概念更接近模块化量子计算——将量子处理器拆分为可协同工作的模块。西方公司已经在这一方向上进行了更大规模的探索：IBM专注于通过经典和量子互联连接超导处理器；QuEra和Pasqal在扩展单一阵列规模的同时开发模块间连接技术；Atom Computing与微软则致力于构建基于网络化量子处理器的集成系统。相比之下，CAS冷原子科技的做法更为紧密，将两个阵列放置在同一台机器内部，而非通过外部互联组成分布式网络。这种高度集成的双核方案是否比单纯扩展单一更大规模的量子阵列更具优势，目前仍是一个开放性问题，而公开的基准测试数据将是回答这一问题的关键。

在应用场景方面，中性原子量子计算机因其较低的运行环境要求和灵活的量子比特操控，在量子模拟、组合优化、化学分子建模等领域具有潜在优势。汉源2号的双核架构有望在特定任务中实现算力拆分或实时纠错，为未来大规模容错量子计算奠定基础。不过，由于性能数据缺失，其实际计算能力和纠错效果仍难以评估。

汉源2号是CAS冷原子科技继首代产品“汉源1号”（Hanyuan-1）之后的迭代机型，但关于第一代系统的技术规格同样极为有限。此次发布虽然展示了中国在量子计算硬件架构上的创新探索，但要证明其在性能和可靠性上具备竞争力，尚需更多公开透明的数据与学术论文支撑。

items.title: AI data center project secretly sucked 29 million gallons of water over 15 months before detected by residents complaining about low water pressure — officials refuse to fine builders of massive 6.2 million-square-foot facility over unauthorized water use
items.description: QTS says the extra 29 million gallons were consumed during temporary construction activities, including concrete work, dust control, and site preparation.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 15 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Fayette County, Georgia, was telling residents to stop watering their lawns to conserve water. The request came after homeowners in a nearby subdivision reported unusually low water pressure, but when the county investigated, it found the cause: a data center campus 20 miles south of Atlanta had been drawing roughly 29 million gallons through two water connections the county didn't know existed, Politico reported Saturday.Quality Technology Services (QTS), the Blackstone-owned developer behind the 615-acre Fayetteville campus, owed $147,474 in retroactive charges for the unmetered consumption, but the county didn’t fine the company.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe QTS Fayetteville (“Project Excalibur”) campus is among the largest data center developments in the country, currently comprising 13 buildings totaling approximately 6.2 million square feet, with plans for up to 16 buildings at full buildout. QTS has said it expects to invest up to $1 billion in the project, which began construction in 2023 and isn’t expected to be completed for another three to five years.Latest Videos From You may like Amazon, Microsoft, and Google under investor pressure to disclose data center water and power use After a $16 billion Stargate AI data center was built despite being voted down, Michigan towns rush to block new buildouts Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center QTS told Politico the 29 million gallons were consumed during temporary construction activities, including concrete work, dust control, and site preparation. The company markets a "closed-loop" cooling system for its data centers, which recirculates the same water rather than drawing from the municipal supply. Once operational, QTS said its facilities would only require water for domestic needs like bathrooms and kitchens.However, the discrepancy between QTS’s stated and actual water usage remained undetected for months, with Politico reporting that the county’s water system director, Vanessa Tigert, attributed the oversight to a procedural error during the county's transition to a cloud-based metering system.Tigert told Politico that her department has a single employee handling both inspections and plan reviews, saying, “... we don’t have enough staff. We can’t keep staff.” QTS and the county disagreed on how long the water went unmetered, withTigert estimating about four months and QTS saying 9 to 15 months. Despite the unauthorized connections, Fayette County opted not to fine the company. "They're our largest customer, and we have to be partners," Tigert said. "It's called customer service."The incident came to light last week after a Fayette County resident obtained the utility's May 2025 letter to QTS through a public records request. Fayetteville had already moved to restrict data center growth before this, with the city council banning new data centers in every zoning district earlier this year, adopting Ordinance 26-0-12. A separate proposal from developer Crow Holdings was denied by the city's planning commission in January, and the company withdrew its appeal in March.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsGeorgia's Public Service Commission also froze Georgia Power's base rates through 2028, specifically to prevent data centers from shifting electricity costs to residential customers. The state is currently experiencing moderate to severe drought, and Gov. Brian Kemp declared a state of emergency last month over wildfires. Georgia hosts more than 200 data center facilities.The QTS campus is projected to generate $150 million to $200 million annually in property tax revenue, according to the city. Fayetteville is one of at least 50 cities across the U.S. that currently have active bans on new data center construction, with four adopting permanent prohibitions, according to the U.S. Data Center Moratorium Tracker. items.titleZh: 人工智能数据中心项目在15个月内秘密抽取了2900万加仑水，直到居民抱怨水压过低才被发现——官员拒绝因未经授权用水对这座占地620万平方英尺的大型设施的建筑商进行罚款
items.descriptionZh:
佐治亚州费耶特县（Fayette County）近日因一个大型数据中心项目引发了供水争议。据Politico报道，该县一处位于亚特兰大以南20英里、由黑石集团（Blackstone）旗下Quality Technology Services（QTS）开发的615英亩数据中心园区（代号“Project Excalibur”），在未经县水务部门知情的情况下，通过两个未被记录的水管连接点，总计抽取了约2900万加仑（约合11万吨）的市政供水。这一异常用水量是在附近住户报告水压异常后，经县方调查才得以发现。QTS为此需补缴147,474美元的水费，但县政府并未对其处以罚款。

该园区是美国当前最大的数据中心开发项目之一，目前包含13栋建筑，总面积约620万平方英尺（约57.6万平方米），远期规划最多扩建至16栋建筑。QTS预计该项目总投资高达10亿美元，2023年启动建设，预计还需3至5年才能完全竣工。QTS对外声称，其数据中心采用“闭环冷却系统”——即冷却水在系统内部循环利用，并不从市政管网取水。但此次被查处的2900万加仑水主要是施工期间的临时消耗，用于混凝土浇筑、扬尘控制和场地准备等工作。QTS强调，园区正式运营后，用水仅会来自洗手间、厨房等生活需求。

然而，这一用水异常在数月内未被发现。费耶特县水务系统主管Vanessa Tigert解释称，这是由于该县在切换至云计量系统时出现了流程错误，且部门人手严重不足——仅有1名员工同时负责巡检和图纸审查。QTS与县政府对未计量取水的持续时间各执一词：Tigert估计约4个月，而QTS称实际为9至15个月。尽管存在未经授权的连接，县政府仍决定不处以罚款。Tigert坦承：“他们是我们的最大客户，我们必须成为合作伙伴。这叫做客户服务。”

该事件于上周曝光，起因是费耶特县一位居民通过公共记录请求获取了水务部门2025年5月发给QTS的追缴函。实际上，费耶特维尔市此前已开始限制数据中心扩张，今年早些时候市议会通过第26-0-12号法令，在所有规划区域禁止新建数据中心。1月份，另一开发商Crow Holdings的提案被市规划委员会否决，该公司于3月撤回上诉。

此事件反映了更广泛的背景：佐治亚州正面临中度至重度干旱，州长布莱恩·肯普（Brian Kemp）上月因野火宣布进入紧急状态；该州拥有超过200个数据中心设施。佐治亚州公共服务委员会已冻结佐治亚电力公司的基础电价至2028年，目的之一正是防止数据中心将用电成本转嫁给居民用户。费耶特维尔市预计，该QTS园区每年可带来1.5亿至2亿美元的地产税收入。

但与此同时，美国全国范围内对数据中心的水电资源消耗正引发日益强烈的反弹。根据“美国数据中心暂停建设追踪”（U.S. Data Center Moratorium Tracker）的数据，全美已有至少50个城市对新建数据中心实施积极禁令，其中4个城市通过了永久性禁令。QTS事件只是冰山一角：此前亚马逊、微软和谷歌等巨头因数据中心用水和用电问题面临投资者要求披露的压力；密歇根小镇因一个160亿美元的“星际之门”AI数据中心不顾投票否决而强行建设，正紧急立法封锁新项目；密苏里州一个小镇则在批准60亿美元AI数据中心后，被居民赶下了市议会。本次事件恰如其分地展示了大型AI基础设施与地方社区资源承载力之间的尖锐矛盾——以及水务监管体系在面对科技巨头时的无力感。

items.title: AI data centers face increasing complaints about inaudible but 'felt' infrasound — citizens complain high- and low-frequency sounds do not register on decibel meters but cause adverse health effects
items.description: Residents living near data centers are raising concerns about noise pollution, as these massive sites operate 24/7 and generate low-frequency sounds that disturb the neighborhood's peace.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Data center projects have faced resistance from residents and communities over their impact on power prices, but another complaint is being raised more frequently — noise pollution. One form of sound pollution is called infrasound, which is inaudible to humans but can be felt, and some claim it causes headaches, insomnia, nausea, and anxiety. Then there's the normal garden-variety sound pollution. The Environmental and Energy Study Institute (EESI), a non-profit organization, said that high- and low-frequency sounds emitted by these industrial sites can be heard and felt for hundreds of feet in surrounding areas, with noise levels reaching as high as 96dB for 24 hours a day and seven days a week.Infrasound is another complaint that researchers are studying. Heatmap Plus reports that this is the phenomenon of frequencies so low they’re inaudible to humans. Nevertheless, some people can feel it, and there have been claims linking them to various negative health effects such as headaches, insomnia, nausea, and anxiety. Infrasound and its effects need further study, but it’s one of the issues local governments have been raising as they place a moratorium on data center projects. If you expand the tweet below, the embedded video includes a recording of the sounds emanating from an AI data center.🦔Residents living near AI data centers are reporting constant low-frequency hum measured as infrasound, sound below the human hearing threshold that causes dizziness, nausea, vertigo, and sleep disruption. The noise comes from cooling systems and onsite gas turbines hyperscalers… pic.twitter.com/6tqjkrGiJ4May 8, 2026Normal noise pollution remains an issue, and communities living near off-grid data centers that generate their own power have it the worst. These sites generate their own power, typically using natural-gas-powered turbines — essentially jet engines bolted to the floor and used to turn generators that produce electricity. Aside from pollution concerns, such as those raised by residents around Elon Musk’s Colossus Supercomputer, which used over 30 mobile gas turbines for power, these turbines can be as loud as a passenger jet, making the site sound as loud as an airport. What’s worse is that, unlike backup generators, which only operate occasionally, these machines run continuously, meaning nearby communities will lose the peace of the neighborhood as long as these data centers operate. You may like AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes — 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects The Controversial Sound Only 2% Of People Hear - YouTube Watch On A single modern AI GPU is estimated to consume up to 3.7 MWh of power annually, and with each server rack containing multiple AI GPUs, that means that it consumes a lot of electricity just to keep it running. This also means they generate a lot of heat — so these systems must be cooled to operate efficiently. Cooling accounts for nearly 40% of data center power use, and it’s also another major source of noise pollution, especially if a site relies on air-cooling. Although a single fan might not make much noise, the scale at which they’re deployed is starting to have a noticeable impact on the sound level they generate.The noise from industrial-grade backup generators compounds this. On-grid data centers require constant power levels to operate at 100%; that’s why most, if not all, of these projects have on-site diesel backup generators. If you have a small diesel generator at home, you know how much noise it makes just to deliver a small amount of electricity. Now, scale that up to data center levels, and you’re looking at multiple generators with massive outputs. The noise output from these machines can reach 105dB, louder than a snowmobile and as intense as a car horn at full tilt.These are meant to run when there’s limited to no power supply, but that is not limited to blackouts. Electricity providers can also reduce the amount of power they deliver to a data center during peak hours, meaning the site’s backup power supply will have to take over to ensure that it operates at full capacity even with less power from the utility company. Aside from that, it must also be tested for up to 50 hours annually, meaning the community surrounding the data center can expect these noises to come on at any time.The United States does not lack flat, open land away from population centers on which to build data centers. However, AI hyperscalers prefer to locate their campuses near existing infrastructure so they don’t have to spend massive amounts of time and resources building everything from scratch. A few data centers are being built on former industrial sites, like shuttered factories and abandoned paper mills, but there are not enough of these around for the number of projects being proposed and built. As the negative effects of building these sites too close to population centers are slowly being revealed, we expect opposition to these projects to keep increasing.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: AI数据中心频遭投诉：听不见但能“感受”到的次声波——市民称高低频声音分贝计不显示却危害健康
items.descriptionZh:
近年来，人工智能数据中心的建设在全球范围内迅速扩张，但其引发的社区矛盾也日益激化。除了此前备受关注的电力价格冲击外，一个更加隐蔽且持续困扰周边居民的问题正被频繁提起——噪音污染。其中，一种名为“次声波”的特殊噪音尤为引人关注：这种频率极低的声音人耳无法直接听到，但人体却能感受到，部分居民声称它会导致头痛、失眠、恶心和焦虑等症状。与此同时，常规的噪音污染也同样严峻。据非营利组织环境与能源研究所（EESI）报告，数据中心发出的高频和低频声音可在周边数百英尺范围内被听见和感受到，噪音水平全天候（每周7天、每天24小时）高达96分贝，堪比繁忙的城市交通或工业机械的持续轰鸣。

次声波现象目前正成为研究热点。根据Heatmap Plus的报道，次声波是指频率低到人耳无法感知的声波，但部分人群确实能“感觉”到它的存在。尽管其与健康问题的因果关系尚需进一步研究，但一些地方政府已开始以次声波问题为由，暂停审批新的数据中心项目。在社交媒体上，有居民分享了AI数据中心发出的持续低频嗡嗡声录音，并将其描述为“次声波”，声称这种声音会导致头晕、恶心、眩晕和睡眠紊乱。这些噪音的主要来源是数据中心的冷却系统以及现场部署的燃气轮机——这些设备本质上就是固定在地板上的喷气发动机，用于驱动发电机发电。

常规噪音污染问题同样不容忽视，尤其对于依赖自发电的离网数据中心而言，情况最为严重。这些站点通常使用天然气涡轮机发电，其噪音水平与客机相当，使得整个数据中心场地听起来像一座繁忙的机场。更糟糕的是，与仅偶尔运行的备用发电机不同，这些涡轮机是持续运行的——只要数据中心在运营，周边社区就永无宁日。例如，埃隆·马斯克的“巨像”超级计算机曾使用超过30台移动式燃气轮机供电，引发了附近居民的强烈抗议。

噪音的另一大来源是冷却系统。现代AI GPU的功耗极高，单个GPU每年耗电可达3.7兆瓦时，而每个服务器机架内安装有多个GPU，意味着巨大的电能消耗和发热量。为了维持高效运行，必须进行强力冷却，而冷却系统占数据中心总功耗的近40%。如果采用风冷方案，虽然单个风扇噪音不大，但大规模部署后，其整体噪音水平已开始对周边环境产生显著影响。

此外，工业级备用柴油发电机的噪音问题也不可忽视。所有并网数据中心都需要持续稳定的电力供应，因此几乎都配备有现场柴油发电机。普通家庭用的小型柴油发电机噪音已经不小，而数据中心级别的多台大功率发电机同时运行时，噪音输出可达105分贝——比雪地摩托更响亮，与汽车全力鸣笛的强度相当。这些备用发电机并非只在断电时运行：电力供应商在高峰时段可能会限制对数据中心的供电，此时备用电源必须接管以维持数据中心满负荷运行；此外，这些发电机每年还需进行长达50小时的测试，这意味着周边社区随时可能被突如其来的轰鸣声打扰。

美国并不缺乏远离人口聚居区的平坦开阔土地用于建设数据中心，但AI巨头们更倾向于将园区选址在现有基础设施附近，以避免从零开始建设的巨大时间和资源投入。虽然已有少数数据中心建在废弃工厂或造纸厂等工业旧址上，但对于当前正在提议和建设的项目数量而言，这类选址远远不够。随着数据中心过于靠近居民区所带来的负面影响逐渐暴露，社区对这类项目的反对声浪预计将持续上升。

items.title: $200 'socketed' Nvidia AI GPU for servers hacked into a PCIe card with custom PCB and 3D-printed cooling — modded Tesla V100 SMX data center GPU runs AI LLMs and is more efficient than many modern midrange offerings in AI inference
items.description: Turns out, Nvidia's older Turing-era V100 AI GPU is still pretty capable today, even with just 16GB of VRAM. A YouTuber got his hands on the SMX variant for just $100, converted it to a PCIe x16 interface for another $100 with an adapter, and got some pretty impressive results across AI inference and NVR benchmarks.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Running LLMs locally on your GPU requires a lot of VRAM, which can drive the rig's cost up exponentially these days. Amidst the ongoing AI boom, the best value lies in older, often forgotten silicon that's still capable, which is exactly what YouTuber Hardware Haven found. He took an Nvidia V100 server GPU with an SMX interface, which is similar to using a socketed processor, and converted it to a standard PCIe bus, which plugged into a consumer motherboard. It ended up performing quite well for its stature (and cost), even against modern SKUs.The contraption begins with an Nvidia Tesla V100 AI GPU that uses the SMX2 socket and is designed for rack-scale deployments. The SMX interface is a mezzanine-based connector that mounts GPUs flat against a specialized baseboard, similar to a CPU socket, and the GPU is then screwed down to the baseboard. The host was able to acquire this GPU for just $100, and the accompanying SMX-to-PCIe x16 adapter was also around $100, bringing the total cost of the setup to $200. The V100 comes with either 16 or 32GB of HBM2 (we're working with 16GB here, sporting 900 GB/s of bandwidth), and it's based on the Turing architecture.The PCIe adapter card didn't come with any cooling of its own, and since the V100 is literally just a heatsink on a PCB, the YouTuber designed and 3D-printed a duct for it. He attached an 80mm Notcua fan on the end to draw in fresh air toward the heatsink. The adapter also has 2x 8-pin PCIe power connectors for, well, power, along with 3x 4-pin PWM headers. It does not feature a secondary SMX socket for NVLink; however, such sockets are much more expensive.Latest Videos From You may like Enthusiast scores watercooled dual Nvidia GTX Titan X GPUs for $86 Intel Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs bring 32GB of RAM to AI and pro apps GPU price tracking 2026: Lowest price on every graphics card from Nvidia, AMD, and Intel today Once the GPU was ready and slotted into a standard Ryzen system, it was time to test just how artificially intelligent a 2017 card is. Keep in mind that the V100 has no display output, so you need integrated graphics in your CPU to actually use your computer. In Ollama, using gpt-oss-20b, the V100 was able to crank out 130 tokens per second, while the Radeon RX 7800 XT in the YouTuber's daily driver system only achieved about 90 tokens per second. This Ridiculous $200 AI GPU Shouldn’t Be This Good - YouTube Watch On Both cards have 16 GB of VRAM, and the RX 7800 XT is even newer with supposedly more efficient silicon, but then again, Nvidia is the gold standard for software support in these benchmarks. So, the host switched to an RTX 3060 12 GB (the best Nvidia GPU he had on hand) to compare against the V100, which is also built on newer Ampere architecture.Running Google's gemma4: e4b, the V100 topped out at 108 tokens per second, while the 3060 12 GB only managed about 76 tokens per second, but it did so consuming less power — 293W on the V100 versus 235W on the RTX 3060. If we calculate tokens per watt, that comes out to around 0.37 for the V100, slightly more efficient than the 0.33 tokens per second per watt on the 3060.Power-limiting the V100 to 100W (it comes with 300W out of the box) dropped the power draw to 170W in the same test, while still producing 95 tok/s. To make the comparison fair, the YouTuber also limited the 3060 to 100W; it ended up consuming 171W and producing just 68 tokens per second. So, with both new results, the V100 achieves an efficiency score of 0.55 tokens/s per watt, while the 3060 12 GB was stuck at 0.39 tokens/s per watt.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsEven though the V100 proved much more efficient overall, despite being several generations old, its idle power draw is the real crux. It sips 45W just sitting doing nothing, compared to 35W on the RTX 3060. Finally, the YouTuber also tested Frigate NVR, which ended up performing really well on the V100, better than the RTX 3060, but consumed more power, as you'd expect.The host's previous setup for Frigate was an Intel-based N100 mini PC that struggled to ever detect his dog on mobilenetv2, but the V100 was able to identify it instantly. Monitoring just two cameras made the V100 pull over 100W, though; the RTX 3060 was similar in this regard, while the older N100 consumed only 26W when operating six different cameras. That marks the end of the benchmarking.This V100 experiment turned out to be a success overall, but the virality of the original video and the fact that we're writing this article mean these bad boys are about to go up in price. So, if you're interested, make sure to grab one before it's too late; the YouTuber found it for just $100 on eBay, and the PCIe adapters for early SMX sockets are cheap enough as well. The 32GB variant of the V100 goes for $500, however. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 200美元的“插槽式”英伟达AI服务器GPU被改装成带有定制PCB和3D打印散热的PCIe卡——改装版Tesla V100 SMX数据中心GPU运行AI大语言模型，在AI推理方面比许多现代中端产品更高效
items.descriptionZh:
YouTuber Hardware Haven近日成功将一块Nvidia Tesla V100服务器GPU通过SMX转PCIe适配器改装到消费级主板上，以仅200美元的总成本（GPU本身100美元，适配器约100美元）实现了超越主流消费级显卡的AI推理性能。这一发现展示了在AI芯片价格水涨船高的当下，被遗忘的旧款高性能计算卡仍具备惊人价值。

V100是Nvidia于2017年发布的深度学习GPU，采用Turing架构，配备16GB或32GB HBM2显存（本次测试为16GB版本，带宽达900 GB/s）。其SMX2接口是一种类似CPU插槽的夹层连接器，原本用于服务器基板上的高密度部署。经过改装后，该GPU通过SMX-to-PCIe x16适配器插入标准AM4主板，配合3D打印的导风罩和80mm Noctua风扇完成散热，并由2个8-pin PCIe电源接口供电（300W TDP），但该适配器不支持NVLink（额外SMX插槽价格昂贵）。

性能测试方面，V100在Ollama上运行gpt-oss-20b模型时达到130 tokens/s，而YouTuber日常使用的Radeon RX 7800 XT（同为16GB显存）仅约90 tokens/s。对比RTX 3060 12GB（Ampere架构），在运行Google gemma4: e4b模型时，V100以108 tokens/s领先3060的76 tokens/s，但功耗更高（293W vs 235W）。计算能效时，V100为0.37 tokens/s/W，略优于3060的0.33。进一步将两者功耗限制至100W后，V100实际消耗170W并产出95 tokens/s，效率提升至0.55；3060消耗171W仅产出68 tokens/s，效率0.39。然而V100空载功耗达45W（3060为35W），是其短板。

在Frigate NVR（视频监控AI检测）测试中，V100能即时识别摄像头中的狗，而YouTuber此前使用的Intel N100迷你PC（仅26W功耗运行6个摄像头）甚至无法稳定检测。但V100仅监控两个摄像头就超过100W功耗，RTX 3060表现类似。

该实验证明V100在LLM推理和计算机视觉任务中效率极高，尤其对比现代消费级显卡时性价比突出。但视频走红后，eBay上这类旧款GPU价格可能上涨（32GB版本已需500美元），有意者需趁早入手。值得注意的是，V100无显示输出，需靠CPU核显驱动显示器。整体而言，对于预算有限但需要本地运行大型AI模型的用户，这种服务器旧卡改装方案提供了令人惊喜的替代选择。

history.title: AMD's excellent Radeon RX 9070 with 16 GB of VRAM hits all-time low pricing — PowerColor Hellhound variant is 23% off list price
history.description: If you've been looking for a GPU upgrade but were disappointed by the ongoing market conditions, a small window of opportunity has just sprung open. AMD's fantastic RX 9070 is on sale for just $554, discounted $165 from its typical retail price. It's a very capable 1440p card that can even game at 4K with enough upscaling.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Thanks to the AI boom causing supply shortages for so many components, it's become a uniquely difficult time to build a PC right now. Indeed, RAM and storage are exorbitantly priced, but even GPUs have gotten more expensive in the past few months. You can still get a good deal here and there, and we've spotted one for you that's hard to miss — AMD's RX 9070 for just $554 on Amazon, down $165 from its typical ask. Check out this deal on Amazon 1440p beastSave 23% ($165.96)PowerColor Hellhound RX 9070 : was $719.99 now $554.03 at AmazonAll-time low price AMD's Radeon RX 9070 is a capable card across almost every resolution. Expect solid 1080p and 1440p performance, with 4K at steady framerates with upscaling. Equipped with 16GB of VRAM, the RX 9070 can run just about every modern title. and even excell in productivity tasks.View Deal This is not the cheapest RX 9070 we've ever seen, but it's close, and it's certainly the most affordable one at this moment. The card itself needs no introduction; we've placed it as the number two pick in our best GPUs roundup for a reason. The 9070 is often outshone by the RX 9070 XT, but don't sleep on this card's value. It can stand its ground against the RTX 5070, delivering buttery-smooth 1440p performance across any game.Featuring 56 Compute Units, paired with 16GB of GDDR6 VRAM across a 256-bit wide bus, the RX 9070 has the same memory bandwidth as the 9070 XT. PowerColor's Hellhound variant has a 12-layer PCB with an 8+1 phase power design, and a dedicated 3+2 phase VRM for the memory. The company is also using PTM7950 on the core, and there's a dual BIOS switch that alternates between an OC and a silent profile. The card doesn't have any RGB because it's going for a sleek and minimal aesthetic.The RX 9070 actually beats the RTX 5070 in rasterization across all resolutions we tested, almost matching the last-gen RX 7900 XT flagship. Similarly, Nvidia's RTX 4070 Ti is weaker than the 9070 overall, while the 4070 Ti Super barely bests it. The story changes when it comes to ray tracing; AMD's offering is definitely better than its previous-gen architecture, but it still falls behind the Green Team, with the 9070 slotting right below the 4070 Super for ray traced workloads. Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) In our review of AMD's 90-class cards this generation, we praised their exceptional price-to-performance ratio, while cutting points for inferior software support. Features like DLSS and RTX HDR remain Nvidia exclusives that might be non-negotiable for some. But for just $554 on Amazon right now, a midrange beast like the RX 9070 is a great deal for someone just looking to play their favorite games during the AI boom. With how uncertain the supply has been for these SKUs, grab yours quick before it sells out. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, Gaming Chair, Best Wi-Fi Routers, Best Motherboard, or CPU Deals pages.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: AMD优秀款Radeon RX 9070搭载16GB显存价格跌至历史低点——撼讯暗黑犬版比建议零售价低23%
history.descriptionZh:
AI热潮导致PC组件普遍短缺，内存、存储和显卡价格飙升，但AMD Radeon RX 9070显卡此时迎来了一波罕见降价。在亚马逊上，PowerColor Hellhound RX 9070售价仅为554美元，较原价719.99美元直降约23%，创下历史新低。这款显卡在1080p和1440p分辨率下表现稳健，配合升频技术也能在4K下维持稳定帧率，16GB GDDR6显存足以流畅运行几乎所有现代游戏及生产力任务。

从核心技术看，RX 9070搭载了56个计算单元（CU），配备256-bit位宽的16GB GDDR6显存，显存带宽与更高端的RX 9070 XT完全一致。PowerColor的Hellhound定制版采用12层PCB板，供电部分为8+1相核心供电加3+2相显存VRM设计，核心导热材料使用了PTM7950相变导热垫，并配有双BIOS开关，可在超频和静音模式间切换。该卡刻意取消了RGB灯效，走极简低调风格。

性能参数方面，在光栅化渲染测试中，RX 9070在全部测试分辨率下均击败RTX 5070，甚至接近上代旗舰RX 7900 XT；相较于NVIDIA的RTX 4070 Ti整体更强，仅略逊于RTX 4070 Ti Super。但在光追性能上，尽管比AMD前代架构有明显进步，仍落后于NVIDIA阵营，大致相当于RTX 4070 Super以下水平。整体而言，RX 9070在本代AMD 90系列显卡中展现出卓越的性价比，但软件生态（如DLSS、RTX HDR等NVIDIA独家功能）仍是短板。

对于在AI热潮中单纯想畅玩主流游戏的玩家而言，这款中端猛兽目前仅554美元的价格极具吸引力。考虑到该型号供货仍不稳定，建议有意向者尽快入手。更多硬件优惠可参阅专业网站的其他显卡、存储、显示器等折扣专题。

history.title: China's Hanyuan-2 debuts as 'world's first' dual-core quantum computer — 200-qubit claims incredible power efficiency, but lacks critical performance benchmarks
history.description: CAS Cold Atom Technology, a Wuhan-based firm affiliated with the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS), unveiled what it calls the world's first dual-core quantum computer.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter CAS Cold Atom Technology, a Wuhan-based firm affiliated with the Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS), unveiled what it claims is the world's first dual-core quantum computer, according to a report from state-owned publication Science and Technology Daily. The system, called Hanyuan-2, pairs two independent neutral atom arrays inside a single cabinet-sized machine, totaling 200 qubits built from 100 rubidium-85 and 100 rubidium-87 atoms.The company said the twin cores can either run in parallel to split workloads or operate in a "one main and one auxiliary" configuration, where the second array handles real-time error correction while the first executes computations. Ge Guiguo, a senior expert at CAS Cold Atom Technology, told Science and Technology Daily that the system represents the first time a quantum processor has moved from single-core to dual-core architecture.Hanyuan-2 is built on neutral atom technology, which traps uncharged atoms using laser arrays to cool and manipulate individual neutral atoms as qubits. CAS Cold Atom Technology's general manager, Tang Biao, said the machine uses a compact, cabinet-style integrated design with a small laser cooling system, with total power consumption sitting below 7 kilowatts.Latest Videos From You may like China announces CPU-only exascale supercomputer with 47,000 homemade processors, record 2 Exaflops of performance without GPUs The future of Quantum computing — the tech, companies, and roadmaps that map out a coherent quantum future Quantum photonics roadmap — how Xanadu and PsiQuantum are looking to transfer qubits through beams of light 200 qubits places Hanyaun-2 well behind the West’s leading neutral atom systems. Atom Computing, for example, demonstrated a 1,180-qubit neutral atom array back in 2023 and has since partnered with Microsoft to deliver error-corrected logical qubits on commercial hardware, while QuEra has delivered error-correction-ready machines to Japan's National Institute of Information and Communications Technology and secured over $230 million in new capital through 2025.Metrics and peer-reviewed papers lackingCrucially, both these Western firms have published metrics like gate fidelity, coherence time, and error rate data for their systems, while CAS Cold Atom Technology has disclosed none of these metrics for Hanyuan-2. No peer-reviewed paper accompanied the announcement either, and, as is usually the case with similar announcements coming out of China, all reporting traces back to Chinese state-affiliated outlets.The use of "dual-core" nomenclature also draws a deliberate parallel to classical multi-core CPUs, but the underlying concept is closer to modular quantum computing, an approach Western companies are already pursuing at larger scales. IBM has focused on linking superconducting processors through classical and quantum interconnect, and QuEra and Pasqal are scaling single arrays while developing inter-module connectivity. Atom Computing and Microsoft are building integrated systems designed around networked quantum processors.CAS Cold Atom Technology's approach is more tightly integrated than a networked architecture, placing both arrays inside one machine. Whether that confers a practical advantage over scaling a single, larger array remains an open question, and one that published benchmarks would help answer.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsHanyuan-2 follows the delivery of the company's first-generation system, Hanyuan-1, though technical specifications for that machine are also limited. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 中国“汉源-2”亮相：号称“全球首台”双核量子计算机，200量子比特宣称能效惊人，但关键性能基准缺失
history.descriptionZh:
中国科学院旗下武汉企业CAS冷原子科技（CAS Cold Atom Technology）近日发布了全球首台双核量子计算机“汉源2号”（Hanyuan-2）。这款系统将两个独立的中性原子阵列集成在一个机柜大小的机器中，共使用200个量子比特——其中100个由铷85原子构成，另外100个由铷87原子构成，实现了双核架构的协同工作。

从技术原理看，汉源2号采用中性原子技术，通过激光阵列捕获不带电的中性原子，并对其进行冷却和操控，使每个原子成为一个量子比特。与传统需要极低温环境的超导量子计算机不同，中性原子系统可在更温和的条件下运行。汉源2号采用了紧凑的机柜式一体化设计，配备了小型激光冷却系统，整机功耗低于7千瓦，在工程集成度上实现了突破。

双核设计的创新在于两种工作模式：一是“并行模式”，两个核心可同时处理不同任务，从而拆分计算负载；二是“一主一辅”模式，一个阵列负责执行主要量子计算，另一个阵列实时进行错误修正，辅助提升计算的可靠性。CAS冷原子科技高级专家葛贵国表示，这是量子处理器首次从单核架构迈向双核架构，意味着量子计算机的系统设计思路开始借鉴经典CPU的演进路径。

然而，在性能参数上，汉源2号与西方领先的中性原子量子系统存在明显差距。例如，美国Atom Computing公司早在2023年就展示了1180量子比特的中性原子阵列，并与微软合作在商用硬件上实现纠错逻辑量子比特；另一家知名公司QuEra已向日本国家信息与通信技术研究所交付了具备纠错能力的机器，并在2025年前累计融资超过2.3亿美元。更重要的是，上述西方企业均已公开其系统的关键指标，如门保真度、相干时间和错误率等数据，但CAS冷原子科技在发布汉源2号时并未披露任何此类指标，也没有提供经过同行评议的学术论文。目前关于该系统的所有报道均来自中国官方媒体，缺乏独立第三方验证。

从架构理念来看，“双核”命名有明显的向经典多核CPU致敬的意图，但其底层概念更接近模块化量子计算——将量子处理器拆分为可协同工作的模块。西方公司已经在这一方向上进行了更大规模的探索：IBM专注于通过经典和量子互联连接超导处理器；QuEra和Pasqal在扩展单一阵列规模的同时开发模块间连接技术；Atom Computing与微软则致力于构建基于网络化量子处理器的集成系统。相比之下，CAS冷原子科技的做法更为紧密，将两个阵列放置在同一台机器内部，而非通过外部互联组成分布式网络。这种高度集成的双核方案是否比单纯扩展单一更大规模的量子阵列更具优势，目前仍是一个开放性问题，而公开的基准测试数据将是回答这一问题的关键。

在应用场景方面，中性原子量子计算机因其较低的运行环境要求和灵活的量子比特操控，在量子模拟、组合优化、化学分子建模等领域具有潜在优势。汉源2号的双核架构有望在特定任务中实现算力拆分或实时纠错，为未来大规模容错量子计算奠定基础。不过，由于性能数据缺失，其实际计算能力和纠错效果仍难以评估。

汉源2号是CAS冷原子科技继首代产品“汉源1号”（Hanyuan-1）之后的迭代机型，但关于第一代系统的技术规格同样极为有限。此次发布虽然展示了中国在量子计算硬件架构上的创新探索，但要证明其在性能和可靠性上具备竞争力，尚需更多公开透明的数据与学术论文支撑。

history.title: AI data center project secretly sucked 29 million gallons of water over 15 months before detected by residents complaining about low water pressure — officials refuse to fine builders of massive 6.2 million-square-foot facility over unauthorized water use
history.description: QTS says the extra 29 million gallons were consumed during temporary construction activities, including concrete work, dust control, and site preparation.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 15 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Fayette County, Georgia, was telling residents to stop watering their lawns to conserve water. The request came after homeowners in a nearby subdivision reported unusually low water pressure, but when the county investigated, it found the cause: a data center campus 20 miles south of Atlanta had been drawing roughly 29 million gallons through two water connections the county didn't know existed, Politico reported Saturday.Quality Technology Services (QTS), the Blackstone-owned developer behind the 615-acre Fayetteville campus, owed $147,474 in retroactive charges for the unmetered consumption, but the county didn’t fine the company.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe QTS Fayetteville (“Project Excalibur”) campus is among the largest data center developments in the country, currently comprising 13 buildings totaling approximately 6.2 million square feet, with plans for up to 16 buildings at full buildout. QTS has said it expects to invest up to $1 billion in the project, which began construction in 2023 and isn’t expected to be completed for another three to five years.Latest Videos From You may like Amazon, Microsoft, and Google under investor pressure to disclose data center water and power use After a $16 billion Stargate AI data center was built despite being voted down, Michigan towns rush to block new buildouts Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center QTS told Politico the 29 million gallons were consumed during temporary construction activities, including concrete work, dust control, and site preparation. The company markets a "closed-loop" cooling system for its data centers, which recirculates the same water rather than drawing from the municipal supply. Once operational, QTS said its facilities would only require water for domestic needs like bathrooms and kitchens.However, the discrepancy between QTS’s stated and actual water usage remained undetected for months, with Politico reporting that the county’s water system director, Vanessa Tigert, attributed the oversight to a procedural error during the county's transition to a cloud-based metering system.Tigert told Politico that her department has a single employee handling both inspections and plan reviews, saying, “... we don’t have enough staff. We can’t keep staff.” QTS and the county disagreed on how long the water went unmetered, withTigert estimating about four months and QTS saying 9 to 15 months. Despite the unauthorized connections, Fayette County opted not to fine the company. "They're our largest customer, and we have to be partners," Tigert said. "It's called customer service."The incident came to light last week after a Fayette County resident obtained the utility's May 2025 letter to QTS through a public records request. Fayetteville had already moved to restrict data center growth before this, with the city council banning new data centers in every zoning district earlier this year, adopting Ordinance 26-0-12. A separate proposal from developer Crow Holdings was denied by the city's planning commission in January, and the company withdrew its appeal in March.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsGeorgia's Public Service Commission also froze Georgia Power's base rates through 2028, specifically to prevent data centers from shifting electricity costs to residential customers. The state is currently experiencing moderate to severe drought, and Gov. Brian Kemp declared a state of emergency last month over wildfires. Georgia hosts more than 200 data center facilities.The QTS campus is projected to generate $150 million to $200 million annually in property tax revenue, according to the city. Fayetteville is one of at least 50 cities across the U.S. that currently have active bans on new data center construction, with four adopting permanent prohibitions, according to the U.S. Data Center Moratorium Tracker. history.titleZh: 人工智能数据中心项目在15个月内秘密抽取了2900万加仑水，直到居民抱怨水压过低才被发现——官员拒绝因未经授权用水对这座占地620万平方英尺的大型设施的建筑商进行罚款
history.descriptionZh:
佐治亚州费耶特县（Fayette County）近日因一个大型数据中心项目引发了供水争议。据Politico报道，该县一处位于亚特兰大以南20英里、由黑石集团（Blackstone）旗下Quality Technology Services（QTS）开发的615英亩数据中心园区（代号“Project Excalibur”），在未经县水务部门知情的情况下，通过两个未被记录的水管连接点，总计抽取了约2900万加仑（约合11万吨）的市政供水。这一异常用水量是在附近住户报告水压异常后，经县方调查才得以发现。QTS为此需补缴147,474美元的水费，但县政府并未对其处以罚款。

该园区是美国当前最大的数据中心开发项目之一，目前包含13栋建筑，总面积约620万平方英尺（约57.6万平方米），远期规划最多扩建至16栋建筑。QTS预计该项目总投资高达10亿美元，2023年启动建设，预计还需3至5年才能完全竣工。QTS对外声称，其数据中心采用“闭环冷却系统”——即冷却水在系统内部循环利用，并不从市政管网取水。但此次被查处的2900万加仑水主要是施工期间的临时消耗，用于混凝土浇筑、扬尘控制和场地准备等工作。QTS强调，园区正式运营后，用水仅会来自洗手间、厨房等生活需求。

然而，这一用水异常在数月内未被发现。费耶特县水务系统主管Vanessa Tigert解释称，这是由于该县在切换至云计量系统时出现了流程错误，且部门人手严重不足——仅有1名员工同时负责巡检和图纸审查。QTS与县政府对未计量取水的持续时间各执一词：Tigert估计约4个月，而QTS称实际为9至15个月。尽管存在未经授权的连接，县政府仍决定不处以罚款。Tigert坦承：“他们是我们的最大客户，我们必须成为合作伙伴。这叫做客户服务。”

该事件于上周曝光，起因是费耶特县一位居民通过公共记录请求获取了水务部门2025年5月发给QTS的追缴函。实际上，费耶特维尔市此前已开始限制数据中心扩张，今年早些时候市议会通过第26-0-12号法令，在所有规划区域禁止新建数据中心。1月份，另一开发商Crow Holdings的提案被市规划委员会否决，该公司于3月撤回上诉。

此事件反映了更广泛的背景：佐治亚州正面临中度至重度干旱，州长布莱恩·肯普（Brian Kemp）上月因野火宣布进入紧急状态；该州拥有超过200个数据中心设施。佐治亚州公共服务委员会已冻结佐治亚电力公司的基础电价至2028年，目的之一正是防止数据中心将用电成本转嫁给居民用户。费耶特维尔市预计，该QTS园区每年可带来1.5亿至2亿美元的地产税收入。

但与此同时，美国全国范围内对数据中心的水电资源消耗正引发日益强烈的反弹。根据“美国数据中心暂停建设追踪”（U.S. Data Center Moratorium Tracker）的数据，全美已有至少50个城市对新建数据中心实施积极禁令，其中4个城市通过了永久性禁令。QTS事件只是冰山一角：此前亚马逊、微软和谷歌等巨头因数据中心用水和用电问题面临投资者要求披露的压力；密歇根小镇因一个160亿美元的“星际之门”AI数据中心不顾投票否决而强行建设，正紧急立法封锁新项目；密苏里州一个小镇则在批准60亿美元AI数据中心后，被居民赶下了市议会。本次事件恰如其分地展示了大型AI基础设施与地方社区资源承载力之间的尖锐矛盾——以及水务监管体系在面对科技巨头时的无力感。

history.title: AI data centers face increasing complaints about inaudible but 'felt' infrasound — citizens complain high- and low-frequency sounds do not register on decibel meters but cause adverse health effects
history.description: Residents living near data centers are raising concerns about noise pollution, as these massive sites operate 24/7 and generate low-frequency sounds that disturb the neighborhood's peace.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Data center projects have faced resistance from residents and communities over their impact on power prices, but another complaint is being raised more frequently — noise pollution. One form of sound pollution is called infrasound, which is inaudible to humans but can be felt, and some claim it causes headaches, insomnia, nausea, and anxiety. Then there's the normal garden-variety sound pollution. The Environmental and Energy Study Institute (EESI), a non-profit organization, said that high- and low-frequency sounds emitted by these industrial sites can be heard and felt for hundreds of feet in surrounding areas, with noise levels reaching as high as 96dB for 24 hours a day and seven days a week.Infrasound is another complaint that researchers are studying. Heatmap Plus reports that this is the phenomenon of frequencies so low they’re inaudible to humans. Nevertheless, some people can feel it, and there have been claims linking them to various negative health effects such as headaches, insomnia, nausea, and anxiety. Infrasound and its effects need further study, but it’s one of the issues local governments have been raising as they place a moratorium on data center projects. If you expand the tweet below, the embedded video includes a recording of the sounds emanating from an AI data center.🦔Residents living near AI data centers are reporting constant low-frequency hum measured as infrasound, sound below the human hearing threshold that causes dizziness, nausea, vertigo, and sleep disruption. The noise comes from cooling systems and onsite gas turbines hyperscalers… pic.twitter.com/6tqjkrGiJ4May 8, 2026Normal noise pollution remains an issue, and communities living near off-grid data centers that generate their own power have it the worst. These sites generate their own power, typically using natural-gas-powered turbines — essentially jet engines bolted to the floor and used to turn generators that produce electricity. Aside from pollution concerns, such as those raised by residents around Elon Musk’s Colossus Supercomputer, which used over 30 mobile gas turbines for power, these turbines can be as loud as a passenger jet, making the site sound as loud as an airport. What’s worse is that, unlike backup generators, which only operate occasionally, these machines run continuously, meaning nearby communities will lose the peace of the neighborhood as long as these data centers operate. You may like AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes — 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects The Controversial Sound Only 2% Of People Hear - YouTube Watch On A single modern AI GPU is estimated to consume up to 3.7 MWh of power annually, and with each server rack containing multiple AI GPUs, that means that it consumes a lot of electricity just to keep it running. This also means they generate a lot of heat — so these systems must be cooled to operate efficiently. Cooling accounts for nearly 40% of data center power use, and it’s also another major source of noise pollution, especially if a site relies on air-cooling. Although a single fan might not make much noise, the scale at which they’re deployed is starting to have a noticeable impact on the sound level they generate.The noise from industrial-grade backup generators compounds this. On-grid data centers require constant power levels to operate at 100%; that’s why most, if not all, of these projects have on-site diesel backup generators. If you have a small diesel generator at home, you know how much noise it makes just to deliver a small amount of electricity. Now, scale that up to data center levels, and you’re looking at multiple generators with massive outputs. The noise output from these machines can reach 105dB, louder than a snowmobile and as intense as a car horn at full tilt.These are meant to run when there’s limited to no power supply, but that is not limited to blackouts. Electricity providers can also reduce the amount of power they deliver to a data center during peak hours, meaning the site’s backup power supply will have to take over to ensure that it operates at full capacity even with less power from the utility company. Aside from that, it must also be tested for up to 50 hours annually, meaning the community surrounding the data center can expect these noises to come on at any time.The United States does not lack flat, open land away from population centers on which to build data centers. However, AI hyperscalers prefer to locate their campuses near existing infrastructure so they don’t have to spend massive amounts of time and resources building everything from scratch. A few data centers are being built on former industrial sites, like shuttered factories and abandoned paper mills, but there are not enough of these around for the number of projects being proposed and built. As the negative effects of building these sites too close to population centers are slowly being revealed, we expect opposition to these projects to keep increasing.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: AI数据中心频遭投诉：听不见但能“感受”到的次声波——市民称高低频声音分贝计不显示却危害健康
history.descriptionZh:
近年来，人工智能数据中心的建设在全球范围内迅速扩张，但其引发的社区矛盾也日益激化。除了此前备受关注的电力价格冲击外，一个更加隐蔽且持续困扰周边居民的问题正被频繁提起——噪音污染。其中，一种名为“次声波”的特殊噪音尤为引人关注：这种频率极低的声音人耳无法直接听到，但人体却能感受到，部分居民声称它会导致头痛、失眠、恶心和焦虑等症状。与此同时，常规的噪音污染也同样严峻。据非营利组织环境与能源研究所（EESI）报告，数据中心发出的高频和低频声音可在周边数百英尺范围内被听见和感受到，噪音水平全天候（每周7天、每天24小时）高达96分贝，堪比繁忙的城市交通或工业机械的持续轰鸣。

次声波现象目前正成为研究热点。根据Heatmap Plus的报道，次声波是指频率低到人耳无法感知的声波，但部分人群确实能“感觉”到它的存在。尽管其与健康问题的因果关系尚需进一步研究，但一些地方政府已开始以次声波问题为由，暂停审批新的数据中心项目。在社交媒体上，有居民分享了AI数据中心发出的持续低频嗡嗡声录音，并将其描述为“次声波”，声称这种声音会导致头晕、恶心、眩晕和睡眠紊乱。这些噪音的主要来源是数据中心的冷却系统以及现场部署的燃气轮机——这些设备本质上就是固定在地板上的喷气发动机，用于驱动发电机发电。

常规噪音污染问题同样不容忽视，尤其对于依赖自发电的离网数据中心而言，情况最为严重。这些站点通常使用天然气涡轮机发电，其噪音水平与客机相当，使得整个数据中心场地听起来像一座繁忙的机场。更糟糕的是，与仅偶尔运行的备用发电机不同，这些涡轮机是持续运行的——只要数据中心在运营，周边社区就永无宁日。例如，埃隆·马斯克的“巨像”超级计算机曾使用超过30台移动式燃气轮机供电，引发了附近居民的强烈抗议。

噪音的另一大来源是冷却系统。现代AI GPU的功耗极高，单个GPU每年耗电可达3.7兆瓦时，而每个服务器机架内安装有多个GPU，意味着巨大的电能消耗和发热量。为了维持高效运行，必须进行强力冷却，而冷却系统占数据中心总功耗的近40%。如果采用风冷方案，虽然单个风扇噪音不大，但大规模部署后，其整体噪音水平已开始对周边环境产生显著影响。

此外，工业级备用柴油发电机的噪音问题也不可忽视。所有并网数据中心都需要持续稳定的电力供应，因此几乎都配备有现场柴油发电机。普通家庭用的小型柴油发电机噪音已经不小，而数据中心级别的多台大功率发电机同时运行时，噪音输出可达105分贝——比雪地摩托更响亮，与汽车全力鸣笛的强度相当。这些备用发电机并非只在断电时运行：电力供应商在高峰时段可能会限制对数据中心的供电，此时备用电源必须接管以维持数据中心满负荷运行；此外，这些发电机每年还需进行长达50小时的测试，这意味着周边社区随时可能被突如其来的轰鸣声打扰。

美国并不缺乏远离人口聚居区的平坦开阔土地用于建设数据中心，但AI巨头们更倾向于将园区选址在现有基础设施附近，以避免从零开始建设的巨大时间和资源投入。虽然已有少数数据中心建在废弃工厂或造纸厂等工业旧址上，但对于当前正在提议和建设的项目数量而言，这类选址远远不够。随着数据中心过于靠近居民区所带来的负面影响逐渐暴露，社区对这类项目的反对声浪预计将持续上升。

history.title: $200 'socketed' Nvidia AI GPU for servers hacked into a PCIe card with custom PCB and 3D-printed cooling — modded Tesla V100 SMX data center GPU runs AI LLMs and is more efficient than many modern midrange offerings in AI inference
history.description: Turns out, Nvidia's older Turing-era V100 AI GPU is still pretty capable today, even with just 16GB of VRAM. A YouTuber got his hands on the SMX variant for just $100, converted it to a PCIe x16 interface for another $100 with an adapter, and got some pretty impressive results across AI inference and NVR benchmarks.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Running LLMs locally on your GPU requires a lot of VRAM, which can drive the rig's cost up exponentially these days. Amidst the ongoing AI boom, the best value lies in older, often forgotten silicon that's still capable, which is exactly what YouTuber Hardware Haven found. He took an Nvidia V100 server GPU with an SMX interface, which is similar to using a socketed processor, and converted it to a standard PCIe bus, which plugged into a consumer motherboard. It ended up performing quite well for its stature (and cost), even against modern SKUs.The contraption begins with an Nvidia Tesla V100 AI GPU that uses the SMX2 socket and is designed for rack-scale deployments. The SMX interface is a mezzanine-based connector that mounts GPUs flat against a specialized baseboard, similar to a CPU socket, and the GPU is then screwed down to the baseboard. The host was able to acquire this GPU for just $100, and the accompanying SMX-to-PCIe x16 adapter was also around $100, bringing the total cost of the setup to $200. The V100 comes with either 16 or 32GB of HBM2 (we're working with 16GB here, sporting 900 GB/s of bandwidth), and it's based on the Turing architecture.The PCIe adapter card didn't come with any cooling of its own, and since the V100 is literally just a heatsink on a PCB, the YouTuber designed and 3D-printed a duct for it. He attached an 80mm Notcua fan on the end to draw in fresh air toward the heatsink. The adapter also has 2x 8-pin PCIe power connectors for, well, power, along with 3x 4-pin PWM headers. It does not feature a secondary SMX socket for NVLink; however, such sockets are much more expensive.Latest Videos From You may like Enthusiast scores watercooled dual Nvidia GTX Titan X GPUs for $86 Intel Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs bring 32GB of RAM to AI and pro apps GPU price tracking 2026: Lowest price on every graphics card from Nvidia, AMD, and Intel today Once the GPU was ready and slotted into a standard Ryzen system, it was time to test just how artificially intelligent a 2017 card is. Keep in mind that the V100 has no display output, so you need integrated graphics in your CPU to actually use your computer. In Ollama, using gpt-oss-20b, the V100 was able to crank out 130 tokens per second, while the Radeon RX 7800 XT in the YouTuber's daily driver system only achieved about 90 tokens per second. This Ridiculous $200 AI GPU Shouldn’t Be This Good - YouTube Watch On Both cards have 16 GB of VRAM, and the RX 7800 XT is even newer with supposedly more efficient silicon, but then again, Nvidia is the gold standard for software support in these benchmarks. So, the host switched to an RTX 3060 12 GB (the best Nvidia GPU he had on hand) to compare against the V100, which is also built on newer Ampere architecture.Running Google's gemma4: e4b, the V100 topped out at 108 tokens per second, while the 3060 12 GB only managed about 76 tokens per second, but it did so consuming less power — 293W on the V100 versus 235W on the RTX 3060. If we calculate tokens per watt, that comes out to around 0.37 for the V100, slightly more efficient than the 0.33 tokens per second per watt on the 3060.Power-limiting the V100 to 100W (it comes with 300W out of the box) dropped the power draw to 170W in the same test, while still producing 95 tok/s. To make the comparison fair, the YouTuber also limited the 3060 to 100W; it ended up consuming 171W and producing just 68 tokens per second. So, with both new results, the V100 achieves an efficiency score of 0.55 tokens/s per watt, while the 3060 12 GB was stuck at 0.39 tokens/s per watt.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsEven though the V100 proved much more efficient overall, despite being several generations old, its idle power draw is the real crux. It sips 45W just sitting doing nothing, compared to 35W on the RTX 3060. Finally, the YouTuber also tested Frigate NVR, which ended up performing really well on the V100, better than the RTX 3060, but consumed more power, as you'd expect.The host's previous setup for Frigate was an Intel-based N100 mini PC that struggled to ever detect his dog on mobilenetv2, but the V100 was able to identify it instantly. Monitoring just two cameras made the V100 pull over 100W, though; the RTX 3060 was similar in this regard, while the older N100 consumed only 26W when operating six different cameras. That marks the end of the benchmarking.This V100 experiment turned out to be a success overall, but the virality of the original video and the fact that we're writing this article mean these bad boys are about to go up in price. So, if you're interested, make sure to grab one before it's too late; the YouTuber found it for just $100 on eBay, and the PCIe adapters for early SMX sockets are cheap enough as well. The 32GB variant of the V100 goes for $500, however. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 200美元的“插槽式”英伟达AI服务器GPU被改装成带有定制PCB和3D打印散热的PCIe卡——改装版Tesla V100 SMX数据中心GPU运行AI大语言模型，在AI推理方面比许多现代中端产品更高效
history.descriptionZh:
YouTuber Hardware Haven近日成功将一块Nvidia Tesla V100服务器GPU通过SMX转PCIe适配器改装到消费级主板上，以仅200美元的总成本（GPU本身100美元，适配器约100美元）实现了超越主流消费级显卡的AI推理性能。这一发现展示了在AI芯片价格水涨船高的当下，被遗忘的旧款高性能计算卡仍具备惊人价值。

V100是Nvidia于2017年发布的深度学习GPU，采用Turing架构，配备16GB或32GB HBM2显存（本次测试为16GB版本，带宽达900 GB/s）。其SMX2接口是一种类似CPU插槽的夹层连接器，原本用于服务器基板上的高密度部署。经过改装后，该GPU通过SMX-to-PCIe x16适配器插入标准AM4主板，配合3D打印的导风罩和80mm Noctua风扇完成散热，并由2个8-pin PCIe电源接口供电（300W TDP），但该适配器不支持NVLink（额外SMX插槽价格昂贵）。

性能测试方面，V100在Ollama上运行gpt-oss-20b模型时达到130 tokens/s，而YouTuber日常使用的Radeon RX 7800 XT（同为16GB显存）仅约90 tokens/s。对比RTX 3060 12GB（Ampere架构），在运行Google gemma4: e4b模型时，V100以108 tokens/s领先3060的76 tokens/s，但功耗更高（293W vs 235W）。计算能效时，V100为0.37 tokens/s/W，略优于3060的0.33。进一步将两者功耗限制至100W后，V100实际消耗170W并产出95 tokens/s，效率提升至0.55；3060消耗171W仅产出68 tokens/s，效率0.39。然而V100空载功耗达45W（3060为35W），是其短板。

在Frigate NVR（视频监控AI检测）测试中，V100能即时识别摄像头中的狗，而YouTuber此前使用的Intel N100迷你PC（仅26W功耗运行6个摄像头）甚至无法稳定检测。但V100仅监控两个摄像头就超过100W功耗，RTX 3060表现类似。

该实验证明V100在LLM推理和计算机视觉任务中效率极高，尤其对比现代消费级显卡时性价比突出。但视频走红后，eBay上这类旧款GPU价格可能上涨（32GB版本已需500美元），有意者需趁早入手。值得注意的是，V100无显示输出，需靠CPU核显驱动显示器。整体而言，对于预算有限但需要本地运行大型AI模型的用户，这种服务器旧卡改装方案提供了令人惊喜的替代选择。
